scenario.context: Station 01: kickoff task and follow-ups for a protection-needs assessment in sales.
scenario.mission: You handle the kickoff task and multiple follow-ups around protection goals, threats, social engineering, storage media, and security measures.

station.profile: CRM, office suite, file storage, and occasional data exchange with external parties

customerRequest.subject: Station 01: assess protection needs in the work area
customerRequest.body:
Please conduct a protection-needs assessment for a standard workstation in the sales area.

After that, additional tasks for the same environment follow with increasing responsibility regarding threats, social engineering, workstation software, and mobile data exchange.

questions.title: Classify protection goals correctly
questions.prompt: Which combination of goals forms the core of information security?

options.text: Confidentiality, integrity, availability
options.rationale: These three protection goals are the recognized base.

options.text: Performance, comfort, design
options.rationale: These are not fundamental information security goals.

options.text: Price, delivery time, brand loyalty
options.rationale: These are procurement criteria, not protection goals.

feedback.correct: Correct: These three goals drive the protection-needs assessment.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: use the classic protection goals.

questions.title: Roles and responsibilities in day-to-day security
questions.prompt: Which statements about roles and responsibilities are technically sound? (Multiple selections)

options.text: The business unit provides process requirements and the view of potential damage
options.rationale: The business unit knows the business impact best.

options.text: IT implements technical protection measures and documents its steps
options.rationale: Technical implementation and evidence typically sit with IT.

options.text: Security activities should be documented as little as possible to save time
options.rationale: Without documentation, traceability and control are missing.

options.text: Reporting paths for security incidents must be known
options.rationale: Fast escalation reduces follow-on damage.

options.text: Each person decides alone on protection needs without alignment
options.rationale: Protection needs require team and process alignment.

feedback.correct: Correct: You separate roles and responsibility in a practical way.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: classify roles, reporting paths, and documentation more clearly.

questions.title: Process of a solid protection-needs assessment
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean protection-needs determination.
items: Derive measures and document residual risk
Identify protection objects and processes
Assess threats and damage scenarios
Classify protection needs with justification
Align results with business unit and IT
correctOrder: Identify protection objects and processes
Assess threats and damage scenarios
Classify protection needs with justification
Derive measures and document residual risk
Align results with business unit and IT

feedback.correct: Correct: The process is methodical and operationally sound.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: move from identification to assessment to aligned decision.

questions.title: Downtime from target availability
questions.prompt: An internal service has a target availability of 99.7% in a 30-day month. How many minutes of downtime are included at most?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Multiply the full time period by the downtime fraction.

questions.title: Brief initial classification note
questions.prompt: Write a short note (4-6 sentences) with protection need, justification, and an initial residual risk.
questions.expectedOutline: Classification, key justification, and a realistic residual point.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is clear, concise, and decision-ready.
feedback.wrong: Please separate protection need, justification, and residual risk more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up 1 - sharpen threat landscape and framework requirements
questions.subject: Please update current threats and security framework
questions.body: Management wants a compact update on laws, standards, and the most important current threats for the sales workstation. Please prioritize the risks traceably.

questions.title: Distinguish laws, standards, and baseline protection
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the difference?

options.text: Laws are mandatory, standards and baseline protection provide structured implementation frameworks
options.rationale: This cleanly classifies the security framework in practice.

options.text: Everything is identical and interchangeable
options.rationale: Laws, standards, and baseline protection have different roles.

options.text: Only internal team rules are relevant for security
options.rationale: External requirements must not be ignored.

feedback.correct: Correct: Mandatory rules and implementation frameworks are clearly separated.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: distinguish legal framework and security standards more clearly.

questions.title: Relevant threats for the sales workstation
questions.prompt: Which scenarios are technically relevant for the described workstation? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Phishing for CRM or mail credentials
options.rationale: Accounts and communication paths are typical attack targets.

options.text: Unauthorized access to quote and customer data
options.rationale: Confidentiality breaches can cause direct business damage.

options.text: Ransomware via email attachment or file storage
options.rationale: This threatens availability and integrity at the same time.

options.text: A new ergonomic office chair
options.rationale: This is not an IT security risk in this context.

options.text: Misconfigured permissions in shared file storage
options.rationale: Wrong permissions quickly lead to unwanted access.

feedback.correct: Correct: You connect the threat landscape with real business impact.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: consider attacks on data, accounts, and permissions more strongly.

questions.title: From security framework to risk priority
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps sensibly.
items: Align measures and feedback with responsible parties
Review policy, rules, and framework requirements
Assess threats and potential damage
Name protection objects and affected processes
Classify protection need and priority with justification
correctOrder: Review policy, rules, and framework requirements
Name protection objects and affected processes
Assess threats and potential damage
Classify protection need and priority with justification
Align measures and feedback with responsible parties

feedback.correct: Correct: The path moves cleanly from framework to prioritized decision.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: first clarify framework and protection objects, then prioritize and align.

questions.title: Calculate risk score
questions.prompt: In a simple matrix: risk score = likelihood (1-5) * impact (1-5). With 4 and 5: what is the score?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Multiply likelihood and impact.

questions.title: Brief recommendation to management
questions.prompt: Formulate a short recommendation (4-6 sentences) with two prioritized threats and a suitable measure.
questions.expectedOutline: Two prioritized risks, a plausible measure, and a brief justification.

feedback.correct: Correct: The recommendation is focused and management-ready.
feedback.wrong: Please link threats, priority, and measure more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up 2 - suspicious contact at the hotline
questions.subject: Possible social engineering attempt reported
questions.body: Someone called pretending to be an external audit body and demanded immediate release of sensitive sales data. Please assess the incident and provide a suitable response for the team.

questions.title: First reaction to a suspicious contact
questions.prompt: What is most sensible in this situation?

options.text: Do not release sensitive data, verify identity via known channels, and report the incident
options.rationale: This keeps verification and reporting under control.

options.text: Under time pressure, share all requested information immediately
options.rationale: This is exactly what social engineering attacks exploit.

options.text: Ignore the call and document nothing
options.rationale: Without reporting, others remain unprotected.

feedback.correct: Correct: Verification and reporting come before speed.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: do not release data without verification and report the incident cleanly.

questions.title: Warning signs for social engineering
questions.prompt: Which indicators point to a social engineering risk? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Pressure to release sensitive data immediately
options.rationale: Time pressure is a classic manipulation pattern.

options.text: Unusual communication channels without verification
options.rationale: Missing verification raises the abuse risk.

options.text: Sender identity does not match the claimed task
options.rationale: Identity mismatches are strong warning signs.

options.text: Multi-step verification via known contact channels
options.rationale: That is a protective measure, not an attack signal.

options.text: A documented approval with a four-eyes principle exists
options.rationale: That reduces rather than increases risk.

feedback.correct: Correct: You identify typical manipulation signals.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: distinguish warning signals from security controls more clearly.

questions.title: Handling a suspicious contact
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for safe handling.
items: Document the incident and inform the team if needed
Do not release sensitive data and end the conversation cleanly
Verify identity via known contact channels
Report the security incident via the known path
Align next steps according to policy
correctOrder: Do not release sensitive data and end the conversation cleanly
Verify identity via known contact channels
Report the security incident via the known path
Align next steps according to policy
Document the incident and inform the team if needed

feedback.correct: Correct: The flow minimizes risk and stays process-compliant.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: sequence protection, verification, reporting, and documentation more cleanly.

questions.title: Reporting rate from a phishing exercise
questions.prompt: Out of 20 employees, 6 correctly reported a suspicious email via the reporting path. What is the reporting rate in percent?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Divide the number of correct reports by the total and convert to percent.

questions.title: Warning message to the team
questions.prompt: Write a short warning message (4-6 sentences) with a verification note, reporting path, and clear behavior rule.
questions.expectedOutline: Name the warning signal, require verification, and clearly state the reporting path.

feedback.correct: Correct: The message is clear, preventive, and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please state verification, reporting path, and behavior rule more clearly.

questions.title: Follow-up 3 - refine protection need for CRM workstation
questions.subject: Please assess protection needs for workstation software and client in more detail
questions.body: Management wants a clearer justification of protection needs specifically for CRM, file storage, and the associated client. Please outline which factors drive the protection need and which minimum measures follow.

questions.title: Most important driver of protection need
questions.prompt: Which factor most strongly determines the protection need of workstation software?

options.text: Sensitivity of processed data and the impact on operations
options.rationale: Data value and business impact largely determine classification.

options.text: Only the color of the software interface
options.rationale: Appearance has no relation to protection need.

options.text: Only the purchase price of the client
options.rationale: Cost alone does not define protection need.

feedback.correct: Correct: Data value and impact are decisive here.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: tie protection need to data and business impact.

questions.title: Aspects for workstation software and client
questions.prompt: Which aspects are especially relevant for the protection-needs assessment? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Processed data category and sensitivity
options.rationale: Data value significantly determines protection need.

options.text: Patch and update capability of software and client
options.rationale: This affects attack surface and operational risk.

options.text: Impact of outage or manipulation
options.rationale: Damage impact is a core element of assessment.

options.text: Only subjective preferences of individual users
options.rationale: Protection need is not a matter of taste.

options.text: Only the purchase price of the client
options.rationale: Price alone does not define protection need.

feedback.correct: Correct: The assessment is based on risk and impact.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: tie data value, operational risk, and damage impact more closely.

questions.title: Assessment for the CRM workstation
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean protection-needs determination.
items: Document minimum protection and residual risk
Record software, client, and data flow
Assess outage, manipulation, and access impacts
Check patch and operational risks of the client
Classify protection need for software and client
correctOrder: Record software, client, and data flow
Assess outage, manipulation, and access impacts
Check patch and operational risks of the client
Classify protection need for software and client
Document minimum protection and residual risk

feedback.correct: Correct: This builds classification in a technical and traceable way.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: first record and assess, then classify and document.

questions.title: Additive priority value
questions.prompt: A simple model adds data value (1-5) and outage impact (1-5). With 5 and 4: what is the total?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Add both factors.

questions.title: Protection-need justification for the CRM workstation
questions.prompt: Formulate a short justification (4-6 sentences) with protection need, a core measure, and a residual point.
questions.expectedOutline: Justify protection need, name the core measure, and make the residual point transparent.

feedback.correct: Correct: The justification is solid and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please make protection need, core measure, and residual point clearer.

questions.title: Follow-up 4 - data exchange via mobile storage media
questions.subject: Please prepare rules for mobile data exchange and a wrap-up note
questions.body: For an external appointment, USB-based data exchange has been requested again. Please assess safe alternatives, the approval process for mobile media, and provide a wrap-up note with residual risk.

questions.title: Best option for sensitive data transfer
questions.prompt: Which option is generally most sensible for sensitive data?

options.text: An approved encrypted transfer path or a managed storage media process
options.rationale: This preserves protection and traceability.

options.text: A private unknown USB stick without approval
options.rationale: That is neither secure nor process-compliant.

options.text: An open messenger without operational approval
options.rationale: Without approval and protection, this is unsuitable for sensitive data.

feedback.correct: Correct: Approved, protected paths are the best choice here.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: sensitive data requires protection and approval.

questions.title: Rules for mobile storage media
questions.prompt: Which rules are sensible for mobile data exchange? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Follow the approval process before use
options.rationale: Use must be agreed and traceable.

options.text: Use appropriate encryption and access controls
options.rationale: This reduces misuse in case of loss or misdelivery.

options.text: Document transport and return
options.rationale: Without evidence, control over the media is missing.

options.text: Test unknown found media directly on a production client
options.rationale: This contradicts any safe handling.

options.text: Prefer secure alternatives such as approved transfer services
options.rationale: Alternatives are often safer and better documented.

feedback.correct: Correct: You connect approval, protection, and traceability.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: prioritize approval, encryption, and secure alternatives more strongly.

questions.title: Safe flow for planned data exchange
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a safe storage media process.
items: Document transport, handover, and return
Clarify data classification and necessity
Select an approved transfer path or storage medium
Apply encryption and access controls
Verify deletion or return after completion
correctOrder: Clarify data classification and necessity
Select an approved transfer path or storage medium
Apply encryption and access controls
Document transport, handover, and return
Verify deletion or return after completion

feedback.correct: Correct: The flow connects protection, evidence, and final control.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: first clarify necessity, then protection, documentation, and closing control.

questions.title: Documentation rate for the storage media process
questions.prompt: In one month there were 12 mobile storage media operations, 3 without documented approval. What is the documented rate in percent?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Divide the documented operations by the total and convert to percent.

questions.title: Wrap-up note for the protection-needs assessment
questions.prompt: Write a wrap-up note (5-7 sentences) with protection need, two core measures, and a remaining residual risk.
questions.expectedOutline: Justified classification, prioritized measures, transparent residual risk, and next step.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is useful for team and leadership.
feedback.wrong: Please structure protection need, measures, and residual risk more clearly.
